title: Per-Cell Beta/Gamma Decomposition

content.hypothesis: Making beta/gamma per-cell arrays (like alpha) enables local adaptation
content.method: Convert beta, gamma from global scalars to per-cell arrays beta[i,k], gamma[i,k]. Adapt via resp_z like alpha
content.result: ALIVE-all (per-cell bg) MI = 0.092 vs ALIVE-alpha-only MI = 0.210. 53% MI loss
content.root_cause: Beta/gamma are structurally global coupling parameters. Per-cell decomposition destroys the coupling mechanism itself

constraints_implied: Per-cell decomposition of global coupling parameters destroys performance (53% MI loss)
Beta/gamma coupling is fundamentally global, not locally decomposable
